source: Adapted from MoWT Vol 7 and iRAP Road Safety Inspection Guidelines for East Africa. Items augmented with East African-specific hazards (boda-boda, pedestrians on carriageway, animal crossings, trading centre speed transitions).

feasibility.description: Review of route options and design concepts. Strategic-level safety assessment.

alignment: Route avoids high-risk terrain (steep escarpments, landslide-prone areas)
Route minimises need for sharp curves or steep gradients
Route avoids or provides for safe passage through trading centres
cross_section: Proposed cross-section width is appropriate for traffic and road users
Provision for NMT facilities considered in cross-section
intersections: Major junction locations identified and type considered
Access management strategy for trading centres
environment: High-risk locations identified (trading centres, schools, markets)

preliminary_design.description: Review of preliminary design drawings and cross-sections.

alignment: Horizontal alignment provides adequate sight distance on curves
Vertical alignment provides adequate stopping sight distance on crests
Combination of horizontal and vertical alignment avoids hidden hazards
Design speed is consistent along the route (no sudden changes)
cross_section: Carriageway width is adequate for design traffic
Shoulder width is sufficient for stopped vehicles and pedestrians
Clear zone is adequate (no fixed objects within recovery area)
Cut and fill slopes are safe (not excessively steep)
intersections: Junction type is appropriate for traffic volumes and turning movements
Junction layout provides adequate sight distance
Pedestrian crossing facilities provided at junctions in trading centres
Boda-boda stage locations identified and separated from junction
speed_management: Speed reduction measures planned for trading centre transitions
Speed is consistent with road function and environment
Advance warning of speed changes provided
nmt: Pedestrian facilities provided through trading centres
NMT conflict points with motorised traffic identified

detailed_design.description: Review of detailed design drawings, specifications, and traffic management plans.

alignment: Horizontal curves have appropriate superelevation and widening
Vertical curves have adequate sight distance for design speed
Sight distance checked at all accesses and intersections
Overtaking sight distance provided on appropriate sections
No hidden dips or crests that obscure road features ahead
cross_section: Carriageway width matches design standard for road class
Shoulder type and width are adequate (paved/gravel, width)
Cross-fall and superelevation provide adequate drainage without skid risk
Roadside clear zone is maintained (forgiving roadside)
Cut slopes and fill slopes are stable and not excessively steep
Guardrails/barriers provided where clear zone is inadequate
intersections: Junction geometry provides adequate sight triangles
Turning lanes provided where warranted by traffic volumes
Pedestrian crossing facilities at signalised intersections
Boda-boda stages located away from junction and carriageway
Access management (control of direct property access near junctions)
roadside_hazards: Fixed objects (poles, trees, bridge piers) are outside clear zone or shielded
Open drainage ditches adjacent to carriageway are protected or covered
End treatments of guardrails are safe (flared or buried)
Utility poles are not in the clear zone
drainage: Drainage structures do not create safety hazards (unprotected headwalls, deep ditches)
Culvert headwalls and wingwalls are outside clear zone or shielded
Drainage gratings do not create bicycle or pedestrian hazards
Water does not flow across carriageway at any point
signs_markings: Advance warning signs provided for curves, junctions, speed changes
Speed limit signs at trading centre entries and repeaters within
Road markings (centre line, edge lines) specified throughout
Sign placement does not obstruct sight distance
Delineation (reflective posts, edge markers) on curves and at hazards
School zone signs and markings provided
nmt: Footpaths provided through all trading centres and built-up areas
Pedestrian crossings at key locations (schools, markets, bus stops)
Crossings have adequate visibility (not on curves or crests)
NMT facilities are continuous (no gaps forcing NMT onto carriageway)
Separation between NMT and motorised traffic is adequate
speed_management: Speed humps/tables at trading centre entries (both directions)
Advance rumble strips before speed changes
Gateway treatments at trading centre entries
Speed design is consistent with function and environment
Speed limit changes are gradual and supported by road design
lighting: Street lighting provided at junctions, trading centres, and pedestrian crossings
Lighting is adequate for night-time NMT visibility
No dark spots between lit areas creating adaptation hazard
east_african_specific: Pedestrian walking space on shoulders assessed for rural sections without footpaths
Boda-boda conflict points identified and managed at all junctions and trading centres
Animal crossing locations assessed (livestock, wildlife) -- warning signs provided
Trading centre speed transition has physical speed reduction measures (not signs only)
Market area encroachment on road reservation assessed for sight distance
Night-time visibility for unlit road users assessed

pre_opening.description: Inspection of completed construction before opening to traffic. Includes day and night inspection.

as_built: Road alignment and cross-section match approved design
Sign and marking installation matches sign plan
visibility: Sight distance is adequate at all junctions and curves (as-built, not drawing)
Vegetation does not obstruct sight distance
Signs are visible and legible from the required distance
Delineation is visible at night (retroreflectivity check)
surface: Road surface is in good condition (no construction defects)
Surface drainage is functioning (no ponding on carriageway)
Skid resistance is adequate (no bleeding or polishing)
safety_devices: Guardrails and barriers are correctly installed and undamaged
Speed humps are constructed to specification
Pedestrian crossings are correctly marked and signed
night_inspection: Road delineation is visible at night from adequate distance
Unlit sections do not create hazards for pedestrians
east_african_specific: Boda-boda stages have been provided with off-carriageway waiting areas
Trading centre speed humps are correctly profiled and signed
Footpaths through trading centres are complete and accessible
Animal crossing warning signs installed at identified locations

existing_road.description: Systematic review of an existing road. Combines crash data analysis with site inspection.

crash_analysis: Crash data reviewed for patterns (location, type, severity, road users)
Vulnerable road user crashes (pedestrian, cyclist, boda-boda) analysed
Time-of-day pattern analysed (day vs night crashes)
alignment: Sight distance adequate on all curves (check for vegetation, structures)
No unexpected sharp curves without advance warning
Vertical alignment does not create hidden hazards
cross_section: Carriageway width adequate for traffic (check for edge deterioration)
Shoulders present and usable (not eroded away)
Roadside clear zone not compromised by structures, poles, trees
intersections: Junction sight distance not obstructed (vegetation, structures, parked vehicles)
Junction type appropriate for current traffic volumes
Pedestrian crossing facilities present where needed
road_condition: Pavement surface condition does not create safety hazards (potholes, rutting, bleeding)
Signs present, visible, and correctly oriented
east_african_specific: Pedestrian walking patterns observed (on carriageway, on shoulder, on footpath)
Boda-boda behaviour observed at junctions and trading centres
Trading centre speed management effectiveness assessed
Market encroachment on road reservation observed
Night-time hazards observed (unlit users, delineation, lighting)
